D9 BiologyL2 UnderstandReference

Fitzpatrick Scale — Types I–VI Skin Phototypes

dermatologist Thomas Fitzpatrick (1975) classified six skin phototypes by UV sunburn vs tan response — the clinical standard for sunscreen recommendation

Type I: always burns, never tans (pale, freckles). Type II: usually burns, tans minimally. Type III: sometimes burns, tans slowly. Type IV: rarely burns, tans well. Type V: tan moderately. Type VI: deeply pigmented, never burns. The scale is used to set sunscreen SPF and skin-cancer risk.

Pick a Fitzpatrick type; swatch + sunburn-risk readout follow.
